24		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`

34		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
35		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
36		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
37		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
38		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
39		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
40		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
41		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
42		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
43		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
44		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
45		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
46		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
47		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.

53		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.

74	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
75	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
76	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
77	// the current attempt.
78	//
79	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
80	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
81	// leaves the bead open.
82	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
83		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
84	}

150	type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions struct {
151		Assignee                string
152		Once                    bool
153		PollInterval            time.Duration
154		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		// HeartbeatInterval, if > 0, overrides bead.HeartbeatInterval for this
157		// worker's claim heartbeat loop. Tests use this to shorten the tick.
158		HeartbeatInterval time.Duration
159		Log               io.Writer
160		// NoReview, when true, skips the post-merge review step even when
161		// ExecuteBeadWorker.Reviewer is configured. Use for doc-only beads or
162		// tight iteration loops where review latency is not acceptable.
163		NoReview bool
164	
165		// ReviewMaxRetries caps the number of reviewer attempts per committed
166		// result_rev before the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required`
167		// event and parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or negative falls back
168		// to DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3). The counter is scoped to a single
169		// result_rev: a fresh result_rev resets the counter, and iterations
170		// without a committed result_rev (--no-merge, execution-failed) do not
171		// consume the budget because the reviewer is not invoked.
172		ReviewMaxRetries int
173	
174		// EventSink receives structured JSONL progress events emitted at
175		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end milestones.
176		// When nil, no structured events are written. Log (terminal text)
177		// is independent and still emitted for human operators.
178		EventSink io.Writer
179	
180		// ProgressCh, when non-nil, receives FEAT-006 ProgressEvents for each
181		// bead execution managed by this loop. The caller is responsible for
182		// draining the channel; the loop sends non-blocking (events are dropped
183		// if the channel is full). The loop does NOT close this channel; the
184		// caller (WorkerManager.runWorker) closes it after Run returns.
185		ProgressCh chan<- ProgressEvent
186	
187		// PreClaimHook, when non-nil, is called before Store.Claim for each
188		// candidate bead. If it returns an error the bead is not claimed and the
189		// loop continues to the next iteration (ctx is NOT cancelled). A nil hook
190		// disables the check. Server and CLI paths wire a real implementation
191		// backed by LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck; tests may inject nil
192		// or a stub that always returns nil.
193		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
194	
195		// Worker/session metadata included in loop.start events so log
196		// aggregators can correlate structured output with the executing
197		// harness/worker. None of these are required.
198		WorkerID    string
199		ProjectRoot string
200		Harness     string
201		Model       string
202		Profile     string
203		Provider    string
204		ModelRef    string
205		SessionID   string
206		LabelFilter string
207	
208		// MinTier and MaxTier bound the tier escalation range when the executor
209		// uses tier-based auto-escalation. Empty string uses the defaults (cheap
210		// and smart). Ignored when the executor has escalation disabled (e.g. an
211		// explicit --harness or --model override was specified).
212		MinTier string
213		MaxTier string
214	}

227	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
228	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
229	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
230	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
231		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
232		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
233		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
234		Now                 func() time.Time
235		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
236		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
237		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
238		Reviewer BeadReviewer
239	}

241	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to opts.ProgressCh non-blocking.
242	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
243	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
244		if ch == nil {
245			return
246		}
247		select {
248		case ch <- evt:
249		default:
250		}
251	}

253	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
254	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) ProgressEvent {
255		return ProgressEvent{
256			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
257			AttemptID: attemptID,
258			WorkerID:  workerID,
259			ProjectID: projectID,
260			BeadID:    beadID,
261			Harness:   opts.Harness,
262			Model:     opts.Model,
263			Profile:   opts.Profile,
264			Phase:     phase,
265			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
266			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
267			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
268			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
269		}
270	}
271	
272	func randomProgressID() string {
273		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
274	}

276	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
277		if w.Store == nil {
278			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
279		}
280		if w.Executor == nil {
281			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
282		}
283	
284		now := w.Now
285		if now == nil {
286			now = time.Now
287		}
288		assignee := opts.Assignee
289		if assignee == "" {
290			assignee = "ddx"
291		}
292		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
293		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
294			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
295		}
296		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
297		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
298			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
299		}
300		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
301		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
302			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
303		}
304	
305		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
306		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
307	
308		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
309			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
310		}
311	
312		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
313			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
314			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
315			"harness":      opts.Harness,
316			"model":        opts.Model,
317			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
318			"assignee":     assignee,
319			"once":         opts.Once,
320		})
321		defer func() {
322			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
323				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
324				"successes":           result.Successes,
325				"failures":            result.Failures,
326				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
327			})
328		}()
329	
330		for {
331			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
332			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
333			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
334			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
335			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
336				return result, err
337			}
338	
339			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
340			if err != nil {
341				return result, err
342			}
343			if !ok {
344				if result.Attempts == 0 {
345					result.NoReadyWork = true
346					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
347						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
348							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
349								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
350								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
351								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
352								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
353								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
354							}
355						}
356					}
357				}
358				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
359					return result, nil
360				}
361				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
362					return result, err
363				}
364				continue
365			}
366	
367			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
368	
369			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
370			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
371			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
372			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
373				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
374					if opts.Log != nil {
375						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
376					}
377					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
378						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
379						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
380					})
381					continue
382				}
383			}
384	
385			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
386				continue
387			}
388	
389			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
390				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
391				"title":    candidate.Title,
392				"assignee": assignee,
393			})
394	
395			if opts.Log != nil {
396				if candidate.Title != "" {
397					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
398				} else {
399					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
400				}
401			}
402	
403			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
404			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
405			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
406			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
407			runStart := now()
408			phaseSeq := 0
409			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
410				phaseSeq++
411				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
412					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
413					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
414				))
415			}
416	
417			nextPhase("queueing", false)
418	
419			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
420			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
421			hbWG.Add(1)
422			go func(beadID string) {
423				defer hbWG.Done()
424				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
425				defer ticker.Stop()
426				for {
427					select {
428					case <-hbCtx.Done():
429						return
430					case <-ticker.C:
431						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
432					}
433				}
434			}(candidate.ID)
435	
436			nextPhase("running", false)
437	
438			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
439			hbCancel()
440			hbWG.Wait()
441			if err != nil {
442				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
443					BeadID: candidate.ID,
444					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
445					Detail: err.Error(),
446				}
447			}
448			if report.BeadID == "" {
449				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
450			}
451			if report.Status == "" {
452				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
453			}
454			if report.Detail == "" {
455				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
456			}
457	
458			result.Attempts++
459	
460			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
461				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
462				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
463				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
464				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
465				reviewApproved := true
466				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
467	
468				if reviewSkipped {
469					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
470						return result, err
471					}
472				}
473	
474				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
475					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
476					if reviewErr != nil {
477						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
478						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
479						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
480						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
481						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
482						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
483						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
484						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
485						attemptCount := prior + 1
486						maxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
487						if maxRetries <= 0 {
488							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
489						}
490						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
491							InputBytes:  0,
492							OutputBytes: 0,
493						}
494						if reviewRes != nil {
495							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
496								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
497								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
498								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
499								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
500								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
501							}
502						}
503						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
504							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
505							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
506								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
507								Summary:   class,
508								Body:      body,
509								Actor:     assignee,
510								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
511								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
512							})
513							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
514							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
515							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
516							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
517							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
518							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
519						} else {
520							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
521							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
522								Kind:      "review-error",
523								Summary:   class,
524								Body:      body,
525								Actor:     assignee,
526								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
527								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
528							})
529						}
530					} else {
531						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
532						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
533						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
534						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
535						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
536						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
537						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
538						// log if the artifact write failed.
539						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
540						if artifactErr != nil && opts.Log != nil {
541							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
542						}
543	
544						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
545							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
546							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
547							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
548							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
549							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
550						}
551						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
552						case VerdictApprove:
553							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
554							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
555							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
556							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
557								Kind:      "review",
558								Summary:   "APPROVE",
559								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
560								Actor:     assignee,
561								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
562								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
563							})
564							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
565								return result, cerr
566							}
567						case VerdictRequestChanges:
568							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
569							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
570							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
571							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
572							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
573								Kind:      "review",
574								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
575								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
576								Actor:     assignee,
577								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
578								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
579							})
580							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
581							if reopenNotes == "" {
582								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
583							}
584							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
585								return result, reopenErr
586							}
587							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
588							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
589							reviewApproved = false
590						case VerdictBlock:
591							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
592							if rationale == "" {
593								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
594									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
595									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
596									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
597									Actor:     assignee,
598									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
599									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
600								})
601								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
602								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
603								report.ReviewRationale = ""
604								reviewApproved = false
605								break
606							}
607							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
608							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
609							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
610								Kind:      "review",
611								Summary:   "BLOCK",
612								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
613								Actor:     assignee,
614								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
615								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
616							})
617							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
618							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
619								return result, reopenErr
620							}
621							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
622							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
623							reviewApproved = false
624						}
625					}
626				}
627	
628				if reviewApproved {
629					result.Successes++
630					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
631				} else {
632					result.Failures++
633					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
634				}
635			} else {
636				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
637					return result, err
638				}
639				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
640					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
641					if cerr != nil {
642						return result, cerr
643					}
644					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale)
645					if aerr != nil {
646						return result, aerr
647					}
648					if satisfied {
649						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
650						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
651						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
652						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
653						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
654						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
655						// satisfied beads).
656						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
657						if evidence != "" {
658							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
659							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
660							report.Detail = evidence
661						}
662						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
663						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
664							return result, cerr
665						}
666						result.Successes++
667						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
668					} else {
669						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
670						// move on to other beads.
671						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
672							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
673							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
674								return result, cerr
675							}
676							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
677						}
678						result.Failures++
679						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
680					}
681				} else {
682					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
683						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
684						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
685							return result, err
686						}
687						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
688					}
689					result.Failures++
690					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
691				}
692			}
693	
694			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
695	
696			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
697			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
698			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
699			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
700			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
701				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
702					return result, err
703				}
704			}
705	
706			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
707			terminalPhase := "failed"
708			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
709				terminalPhase = "done"
710			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
711				terminalPhase = "preserved"
712			}
713			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
714			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
715			if finalAttemptID == "" {
716				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
717			}
718			phaseSeq++
719			emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
720				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
721				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
722				WorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,
723				ProjectID: opts.ProjectRoot,
724				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
725				Harness:   opts.Harness,
726				Model:     opts.Model,
727				Profile:   opts.Profile,
728				Phase:     terminalPhase,
729				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
730				Heartbeat: false,
731				TS:        now().UTC(),
732				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
733				Message:   report.Detail,
734			})
735	
736			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
737				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
738				"status":               report.Status,
739				"detail":               report.Detail,
740				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
741				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
742				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
743				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
744				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
745				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
746			})
747	
748			if opts.Log != nil {
749				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
750			}
751	
752			if opts.Once {
753				return result, nil
754			}
755		}
756	}

758	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
759		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
760		if err != nil {
761			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
762		}
763		for _, candidate := range ready {
764			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
765				continue
766			}
767			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
768				continue
769			}
770			return candidate, true, nil
771		}
772		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
773	}

775	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
776	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
777	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
778	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
779	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
780		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
781			return
782		}
783		provider := report.Provider
784		if provider == "" {
785			provider = report.Harness
786		}
787		if provider == "" {
788			return
789		}
790		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
791			"resolved_provider": provider,
792			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
793			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
794			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
795			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
796			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
797			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
798			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
799		})
800		if err != nil {
801			return
802		}
803		summary := "provider=" + provider
804		if report.Model != "" {
805			summary += " model=" + report.Model
806		}
807		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
808			Kind:      "routing",
809			Summary:   summary,
810			Body:      string(body),
811			Actor:     "ddx",
812			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
813			CreatedAt: createdAt,
814		})
815	}

817	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
818		parts := []string{}
819		if report.Detail != "" {
820			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
821		}
822		if report.Tier != "" {
823			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
824		}
825		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
826			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
827		}
828		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
829			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
830		}
831		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
832			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
833		}
834		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
835			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
836		}
837		if report.ResultRev != "" {
838			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
839		}
840		if report.BaseRev != "" {
841			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
842		}
843		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
844			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
845		}
846	
847		return bead.BeadEvent{
848			Kind:      "execute-bead",
849			Summary:   report.Status,
850			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
851			Actor:     actor,
852			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
853			CreatedAt: createdAt,
854		}

857	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
858	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
859	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
860	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
861	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
862	// never break the core execute-loop.
863	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
864		if sink == nil {
865			return
866		}
867		if data == nil {
868			data = map[string]any{}
869		}
870		entry := map[string]any{
871			"session_id": sessionID,
872			"type":       eventType,
873			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
874			"data":       data,
875		}
876		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
877		if err != nil {
878			return
879		}
880		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
881		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
882	}

884	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
885	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
886	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
887		switch report.Status {
888		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
889			shortRev := report.ResultRev
890			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
891				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
892			}
893			if shortRev != "" {
894				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
895			}
896			return "merged"
897		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
898			return "already_satisfied"
899		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
900			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
901				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
902			}
903			return "no_changes"
904		default:
905			detail := report.Detail
906			if detail == "" {
907				detail = report.Status
908			}
909			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
910				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
911			}
912			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
913		}
914	}
915	
916	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
917	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
918	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
919	
920	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
921	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
922	
923	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
924	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
925	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
926	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
927	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
928		if rationale == "" {
929			return false
930		}
931		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
932	}
933	
934	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
935	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
936	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
937	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
938	//
939	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
940	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
941	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
942	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
943	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale string) (bool, string, error) {
944		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
945			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
946		}
947		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
948			evidence := rationale
949			return true, evidence, nil
950		}
951		if noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose {
952			return true, fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount), nil
953		}
954		return false, "", nil
955	}
956	
957	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
958		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
959			return false
960		}
961		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
962	}
963	
964	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
965	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
966	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
967	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
968	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
969	//     embed the class in their message).
970	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
971	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
972	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
973	//     matches.
974	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
975		if reviewRes != nil {
976			switch reviewRes.Error {
977			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
978				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
979				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
980				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
981				return reviewRes.Error
982			}
983		}
984		msg := ""
985		if reviewErr != nil {
986			msg = reviewErr.Error()
987		}
988		switch {
989		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
990			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
991		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
992			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
993		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
994			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
995		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
996			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
997		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
998			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
999		}
1000		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1001	}
1002	
1003	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1004	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1005	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1006	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1007	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1008	
1009	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1010	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1011	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1012	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1013	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1014		if resultRev == "" {
1015			return 0
1016		}
1017		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1018		if err != nil {
1019			return 0
1020		}
1021		n := 0
1022		for _, ev := range events {
1023			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1024				continue
1025			}
1026			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1027			if m == nil {
1028				continue
1029			}
1030			if m[1] == resultRev {
1031				n++
1032			}
1033		}
1034		return n
1035	}
1036	
1037	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1038	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1039	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1040	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1041	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1042	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1043		var b strings.Builder
1044		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1045		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1046		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1047		if message != "" {
1048			b.WriteString("\n")
1049			b.WriteString(message)
1050		}
1051		return b.String()
1052	}
1053	
1054	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1055		if d <= 0 {
1056			return nil
1057		}
1058		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1059		defer timer.Stop()
1060		select {
1061		case <-ctx.Done():
1062			return ctx.Err()
1063		case <-timer.C:
1064			return nil
1065		}
1066	}

9	# Solution Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	Eliminate the manual command-layer wiring pattern that copies config
14	fields into options structs at every dispatch site. Replace it with a
15	type-system-enforced contract where every worker, runner, and review
16	path receives a single resolved configuration object that the type
17	system requires. Make the bug class — "config field exists but no
18	caller wires it through, so the loop silently uses defaults" — locally
19	unreachable.
20	
21	## Scope
22	
23	In scope:
24	
25	- Architectural pattern: `ResolvedConfig` (immutable) + per-family
26	  `*Runtime` structs replace the `*Options` family today.
27	- Three production dispatch sites that must call `config.Load`:
28	  CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`.
29	- Five options families that get migrated in stages:
30	  `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`, `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`,
31	  `CompareOptions`, `QuorumOptions`.
32	- Three latent dead-config-field cases that surface during the
33	  refactor: `NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, and
34	  `HeartbeatInterval` are hardcoded constants today; `ContextBudget`
35	  has no config home; `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` is parsed but never
36	  consumed.
37	- Lint enforcement (Stage 4) that mechanically forbids the old
38	  `*Options`-with-durable-fields pattern.
39	- Test coverage uplift: the four disabled config tests
40	  (`*.disabled`) get evaluated and either restored against the new
41	  API or deleted with rationale.
42	
43	Out of scope:
44	
45	- The CONTRACT-003 boundary with `~/Projects/agent`. Investigation
46	  confirmed the agent module does not import `ddx/internal/config`
47	  and ddx pre-resolves at `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:104`
48	  and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:80`. No agent-repo changes
49	  are required and no contract document needs revision. Agent
50	  v0.9.9 (ADR-005 smart routing) further simplifies the boundary by
51	  removing `ServiceExecuteRequest.PreResolved` — `ResolveRoute` is
52	  now informational and `Execute` re-resolves on its own inputs.
53	  The ddx side just populates `EstimatedPromptTokens` and
54	  `RequiresTools` (new per-invocation hints) on `ServiceExecuteRequest`;
55	  routing decisions are owned upstream.
56	- Routing-layer cleanup driven by agent v0.9.9 (rendering composed
57	  inventory in `ddx agent route-status`, retiring `agent.routing.model_overrides`,
58	  dropping the "No model routes configured" fatal error path).
59	  Tracked separately as a small follow-up batch on top of FEAT-006;
60	  does not block this refactor and is not delivered by it.
61	- The wire shape of `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` /
62	  `agentlib.ExecuteResponse`. The refactor changes how ddx-side state
63	  is organized before the request is built; the request itself is
64	  unchanged.
65	- New CLI commands or operator workflows. This is a structural
66	  refactor; user-visible behavior is preserved.
67	
68	## Problem
69	
70	A reviewer recently caught that FEAT-022 Stage G shipped a
71	`ReviewMaxRetries` field on `*config.Config` plus a resolver, and an
72	`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.ReviewMaxRetries` field that the loop honors,
73	but no caller in `cli/cmd/` ever read the resolved value and assigned
74	it to the options struct. The config field was dead in production. The
75	test that claimed to verify the loop "respects" the override actually
76	injected the field directly into options.
77	
78	Investigation found this is systemic, not a one-off:
79	
80	- 6 production sites that construct `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
81	  `RunOptions`, or `ExecuteBeadOptions` across CLI and server.
82	  **Zero** of them call `config.Load` before constructing the options
83	  struct.
84	- Three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work` at
85	  `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997`, server `runWorker` at
86	  `cli/internal/server/workers.go:878`, GraphQL `StartWorker` at
87	  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:77`) all skip
88	  config entirely and populate options from CLI flags or request
89	  fields only. Every config-derived knob across every options struct
90	  is dead in the same way `ReviewMaxRetries` was.
91	- Two more `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` fields, `NoProgressCooldown` and
92	  `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, are hardcoded constants in
93	  `execute_bead_loop.go:292` with no config field at all. They have
94	  the same shape as `ReviewMaxRetries` would have had if no resolver
95	  existed.
96	- The Go type system permits constructing any `*Options` struct with
97	  zero-value defaults. The compiler cannot tell the implementer
98	  "you forgot to wire this field." Every new config knob is a new
99	  opportunity for the same bug.
100	
101	The pattern accumulated weeks of cruft: each new config knob added a
102	field to `*Options`, a resolver to `*Config`, and silently relied on
103	every caller remembering to bridge them. The reviewer caught one
104	instance because Stage G's acceptance criteria explicitly demanded an
105	end-to-end YAML→loop test. Without that explicit gate, the gap stays
106	invisible.
107	
108	## Approach
109	
110	Three structural changes that compose:
111	
112	### 1. `ResolvedConfig` is the only valid input.
113	
114	A new immutable value type `config.ResolvedConfig` exposes accessors
115	for every durable knob a worker, runner, or review path consumes. The
116	type has no exported fields; clients call methods.
117	
118	A naive "unexported fields" implementation does not actually prevent
119	construction — Go's zero-value rule means
120	`var rcfg config.ResolvedConfig` and `config.ResolvedConfig{}` are
121	both valid expressions in any package. The design closes this escape
122	hatch with a sealed-construction pattern: `ResolvedConfig` carries a
123	single unexported sentinel field set only by `Resolve`. Every public
124	accessor checks the sentinel on the first read and panics with a
125	specific message naming `LoadAndResolve` if the sentinel is unset.
126	A zero-value `ResolvedConfig` thus passes the type check but fails
127	loudly on any access — making the bypass unreachable in any code
128	path that actually consumes the value.
129	
130	`ResolvedConfig` is shared across goroutines without synchronization
131	because no method mutates it. There is no `Set*` API, no public
132	mutator, no JSON unmarshal target.
133	
134	### 2. Runtime structs hold runtime plumbing only.
135	
136	`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` becomes `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, with all
137	durable fields removed. The same applies to the four other options
138	families. A runtime struct holds:
139	
140	- non-serializable plumbing (`Log io.Writer`, `EventSink`,
141	  `ProgressCh`, `PreClaimHook`)
142	- per-invocation runtime intent (`Once`, `LabelFilter`, `SessionID`,
143	  `WorkerID`)
144	
145	Anything that could plausibly live in config — anything someone might
146	want to set persistently for a project — is forbidden from the runtime
147	struct. The compiler enforces this because the durable fields are
148	removed entirely.
149	
150	Run, RunCompare, RunQuorum, ExecuteBead, and ReviewBead all change
151	signature to:
152	
153	```go
154	func (...) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime XRuntime) (...)
155	```
156	
157	### 3. All three dispatch sites resolve once, hand off everywhere — atomically.
158	
159	The three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`,
160	GraphQL `StartWorker`) all gain a single shared resolution helper:
161	
162	```go
163	rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
164	```
165	
166	`overrides` carries CLI flag values or request fields. The helper
167	loads the project's `.ddx/config.yaml`, applies overrides as a layer,
168	and returns the immutable `ResolvedConfig`. Every downstream call
169	takes that single value.
170	
171	**All three sites must be migrated together for the bug class to
172	actually close.** Migrating only the CLI site leaves `ddx work` from
173	a server context still bypassing config (which is what production
174	actually uses). Migrating only the server site leaves direct CLI
175	runs broken. The migration discipline (see §Migration) requires that
176	the loop migration be considered "complete" only when all three
177	dispatch paths route through `LoadAndResolve`.
178	
179	There is no other supported way to obtain a `ResolvedConfig` in
180	production code. Adding a fourth dispatch entry (e.g. a future REST
181	endpoint that starts a worker) requires calling `LoadAndResolve` at
182	that site.
183	
184	## Why this approach
185	
186	Three alternatives considered and rejected:
187	
188	**Alternative A: builder pattern on options struct.**
189	`agent.NewExecuteBeadLoopOptions(cfg).WithProject(p)`.
190	Surface looks cleaner but does not eliminate the bug class — the
191	builder still has setters per durable field, and a new config field
192	still requires updating the builder. Test code can still call
193	`builder.WithReviewMaxRetries(0)` to bypass real config. Does not
194	make the bypass impossible.
195	
196	**Alternative B: documented immutability of `*Config` shared across
197	callers.** Cheaper to implement (no new type) but defeated by Go's
198	shallow-copy semantics on structs containing maps and pointers.
199	`*Config` carries `PersonaBindings map`, `*AgentConfig` (with
200	`Models map`), `*RoutingConfig` (multiple maps), and
201	`*EvidenceCapsConfig` (`PerHarness map`). A shallow copy aliases all
202	these maps. A caller that runs `cfg2 := *cfg; cfg2.AgentConfig.Models[k] = v`
203	silently mutates the original. Documentation is not enforcement.
204	The type system has to make the failure mode unreachable.
205	
206	**Alternative C: pass `*Config` directly with the existing `*Options`
207	shape preserved.** Would require every caller to read `cfg.Resolve*`
208	methods and copy results into options. This is what we have today.
209	The bug class persists; we only delay it.
210	
211	The chosen approach makes three things simultaneously true:
212	
213	1. The compiler refuses to call `Run` without a `ResolvedConfig`.
214	2. The runtime struct cannot carry a durable field, so a future config
215	   knob has nowhere to bypass to.
216	3. There is exactly one production path that produces a
217	   `ResolvedConfig`, so adding a new config knob requires updating
218	   exactly one place (the `Resolve` method) for it to be visible to
219	   every consumer.
220	
221	## Migration discipline
222	
223	The refactor delivers across many small beads, not a few big stages.
224	Two non-negotiable principles govern every bead:
225	
226	**Green tree.** Every bead leaves the tree fully functional. No bead
227	breaks user-visible behavior — `ddx work` keeps draining the queue,
228	`ddx agent run` keeps dispatching, the server keeps accepting GraphQL
229	mutations. The migration adds new types and call paths *alongside*
230	the existing ones, then retires the old ones in dedicated cleanup
231	beads only after every caller has moved.
232	
233	**Per-bead size budget.** Each bead must fit in a single execute-bead
234	worker window: target <500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent
235	time, under $10 of model spend. Empirically, FEAT-022's beads averaged
236	7-15 minutes and $3-7. Anything that wants to "do the whole loop
237	migration in one shot" is sized wrong and must be split.
238	
239	These principles imply a specific implementation pattern at every
240	seam:
241	
242	1. **Add new alongside old.** Introduce `ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`,
243	   `LoadAndResolve`, the `*Runtime` structs, and the new `Run`
244	   signatures *alongside* the existing `*Options` types and `Run`
245	   methods. Old methods stay alive and call into the new path
246	   internally if needed; production callers haven't moved yet.
247	2. **Migrate one production caller per bead.** Each dispatch site
248	   moves in its own bead, with a behavioral test that proves config
249	   actually drives the resulting behavior at that site.
250	3. **Migrate test files in groups.** Test sites are mechanical;
251	   group them by file (one test file per bead). Tests on the old
252	   `*Options` API stay green via a temporary shim until the file is
253	   migrated.
254	4. **Retire old types in dedicated cleanup beads.** When and only
255	   when every production and test caller has moved, a final bead
256	   removes the old type and shim.
257	
258	## Stage groupings
259	
260	The bead inventory is structured into stage groupings. Each grouping
261	is a logical milestone, not a single bead. The actual bead count
262	exceeds 25 — see TD-024 §Bead Inventory for the per-bead breakdown.
263	
264	Stage 1 unblocks FEAT-022 Stage G. Stages 2–4 land sequentially or
265	with overlap.
266	
267	**Stage 1 — Foundation + ExecuteBeadLoop.**
268	Introduces `ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`, `CLIOverrides`,
269	`LoadAndResolve`, the test-config constructors, and the
270	`ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` shape. Adds the new `*Runtime` types and
271	new `RunWithConfig` methods *alongside* existing `*Options` types
272	and `Run` methods (green-tree principle). Adds resolvers for
273	`NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, `HeartbeatInterval`.
274	Migrates **all three production dispatch sites** (CLI `ddx work`,
275	server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`) plus ~44 test sites.
276	Retires the legacy `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` + `Run` once all
277	callers migrate. Adds behavioral e2e tests on every dispatch path
278	proving `.ddx/config.yaml` actually drives the running loop. This
279	stage blocks FEAT-022 Stage G.
280	
281	**Stage 2 — RunOptions migration.**
282	Migrates `RunOptions` → `AgentRunRuntime` using the same
283	add-alongside-then-retire pattern. ~14 constructor sites including
284	`CompareOptions` and `QuorumOptions` (which embed `RunOptions`).
285	New test-config constructor variant for the run path.
286	
287	**Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.**
288	Migrates `ExecuteBeadOptions` → `ExecuteBeadRuntime`. ~16 sites.
289	Adds `ContextBudget` to `EvidenceCapsConfig` (currently has no
290	config home). Wires `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` (currently parsed but
291	not consumed) into the resolved-config path.
292	
293	**Stage 4 — Lint enforcement and test debt cleanup.**
294	Adds a structural lint analyzer (`runtimelint`) using a closed-list
295	of forbidden field names scoped to `*Runtime` structs in
296	`cli/internal/agent/`. Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022
297	Stage A2 so the new rule plugs into the same Lefthook + CI hooks.
298	Resolves the four disabled config tests per evidence-driven
299	decisions specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution: two are
300	deleted (their target features are dead in the current loader),
301	two are rewritten against the current API.
302	
303	## Test coverage commitment
304	
305	Stage 1's e2e tests are non-negotiable. Each must:
306	
307	1. Load a real `.ddx/config.yaml` from disk that sets a non-default
308	   value for the knob under test.
309	2. Pass through `LoadAndResolve` (no test-injected options struct).
310	3. Execute the production code path (real `Run`, not a mock).
311	4. Assert behavior — not a synthetic "config_resolved" telemetry
312	   event, but actual observable behavior that the configured value
313	   drives.
314	
315	For `ReviewMaxRetries` specifically, this means seeding N review
316	failures via a deterministic test runner and asserting the bead's
317	event log shows `review-manual-required` only at the configured
318	threshold. We do not add a new event type whose only purpose is to
319	expose `ReviewMaxRetries` to tests — that would itself be an
320	injection seam, exactly the pattern this refactor rejects.
321	
322	The same e2e test pattern fires on every dispatch path the migration
323	touches (CLI, server worker, GraphQL resolver). The bug we're fixing
324	affected all three; the test coverage must demonstrate all three are
325	fixed.
326	
327	Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
328	either return to the suite (against the new API), get deleted with
329	a one-line rationale, or get rewritten if the underlying feature is
330	alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4
331	must equal or exceed the pre-refactor count plus the new e2e and
332	accessor tests. Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files
333	are specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution.
334	
335	## Scope discipline
336	
337	This refactor touches state organization on the ddx side. It does not
338	touch:
339	
340	- `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` or `agentlib.ExecuteResponse` shape.
341	- Any code in `~/Projects/agent`.
342	- The session log format (FEAT-006, TD-006).
343	- The execution bundle layout (TD-010).
344	- The bead tracker schema (SD-004, TD-004).
345	- The model catalog (SD-014).
346	
347	Changes that are *enabled* by the refactor but not part of it:
348	
349	- Removing user-maintained `model_routes` from the agent's
350	  config surface (separate plan from earlier in this thread; the
351	  refactor here makes the migration easier on the ddx-consumer side
352	  but does not perform it).
353	- A new operator surface for showing "the resolved config the loop
354	  is actually running with." Worth doing but tracked separately.
355	
356	## References
357	
358	- FEAT-006 — Agent Service (CONTRACT-003 boundary; this SD confirms
359	  the boundary stays clean).
360	- FEAT-022 — Prompt Evidence Assembly. This SD's Stage 1 unblocks
361	  FEAT-022 Stage G.
362	- SD-019 — Multi-Project Server Topology. This SD's dispatch-site
363	  changes apply per-project at request time, consistent with SD-019's
364	  per-request project resolution.
365	- TD-024 — Technical Design counterpart specifying the concrete
366	  type definitions, accessor signatures, and migration code shapes.

5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}
23	
24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
56	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
57	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
58	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
59	// applied at Resolve time only.
60	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
61		return CLIOverrides{
62			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
63			Profile:  opts.Profile,
64			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
65			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
66		}
67	}
